status_at_close: RESOLVED — Option A applied; both tests relabeled as Cat-B carry-forwards. Threshold raised 33->35.
operator_decision: Option A — relabel + raise threshold + defer to follow-on
follow_ups: vuln-source-parity-v1 absorbs test_vuln_detects_xss (Python scope expansion — operator review at M5 to confirm milestone scope vs. tiny dedicated patch milestone)
ruby-bare-call-extraction-v1 (TBD follow-on) addresses bare-`gets` AST limitation in tree-sitter-ruby (parses bareword `gets` as identifier, not call) — closes the documented FAI-v1 M5 carry-forward
operator_rationale_test_1_test_vuln_dete: Genuine vuln-migration-v1 M2/M3 coverage gap. AST sink bank doesn't cover f-string return from `@app.route` decorated function as HtmlOutput. This is exactly the class vuln-source-parity-v1 was designed to absorb (1 lang scope expansion — Python wasn't in the original 6, but the milestone scope is 'extend canonical AST source/sink banks to close the M3-CF-01 carry-forward class').
operator_rationale_test_2_ruby_io_popen: NOT a fresh regression. This is the documented FAI-v1 M5 carry-forward (commit 7849b3f). FAI-v1 M5 explicitly retained `\bgets\b` regex in RUBY_PATTERNS.sources as 'Option A' because tree-sitter-ruby parses bare `gets` as identifier (not call), so AST `call_names: ['gets']` can't cover it. Under analyze_ast_only (the test harness FAI-v1 M1 added), the regex bank is short-circuited — so the test fails BY DESIGN. Has been failing since FAI-v1 M5 shipped. M1 enumeration missed cataloging it as Cat-B; that's the gap.
suspected_commits: 8d25e8c feat(vuln-migration-v1 M3): collapse vuln.rs onto canonical compute_taint_with_tree
870f03e feat(vuln-migration-v1 M2): extend AST sink banks for 4 new VulnTypes across 16 langs
cb32c12 feat(vuln-migration-v1 M1): RED scaffold + TaintSinkType +4 variants + perf baseline
diagnosis:
test_vuln_detects_xss creates a Python file with two Flask view functions using f-strings that interpolate request.args.get / request.form.get into HTML strings (and one wrapped in render_template_string). Per `tldr taint` analysis, the user-input source (name = request.args.get('name', 'Guest')) IS detected and the variable IS tracked as tainted across the function. However, NO sinks are detected: the f-string return value `f"

Hello, {name}!

"` is not recognized as an HtmlOutput sink (which would map to VulnType::Xss). Result: taint analysis reports zero flows -> vuln command reports zero findings -> test fails (`assert!(!xss_findings.is_empty(), "Should detect XSS")`).
root_cause_hypothesis:
The vuln-migration-v1 M2/M3 work migrated regex sink detection to AST sink detection via TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput. The new AST sink banks (per crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs L1711-1718, L2016-2036, L2680-2687, L2968-2978) cover render_template_string-style call sinks but appear NOT to cover the case where a Python view function `return`s an f-string containing tainted variables. The greet() route's `return f"

Hello, {name}!

"` carries `name` (tainted) into the HTTP response body — that's the XSS sink — but the AST walker doesn't recognize the f-string-as-return-value as an HtmlOutput sink.
scope_decision: Restoring this XSS detection requires modifying the active vuln/taint subcommand handler — specifically extending the Python AST sink bank in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs to recognize f-string returns from view functions (or any function returning a tainted string from a route-decorated function) as HtmlOutput. This is OUT OF SCOPE for workspace-test-infrastructure-v1 M4 per dispatch-contract validator mandates: (1) `no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations` — taint.rs sink banks are not in the M4 named exception list (cognitive analyzer, parse_coverage, analyze_dead_code, compute_martin_metrics, git_log, change-impact NoBaseline reason), and (2) `operator_escalation_on_regression_from_prior_milestone` — this regression was introduced by vuln-migration-v1 (which was supposed to maintain parity); a milestone-revert decision belongs to the operator, not to M4.
recommendation: Defer this fix to a follow-up vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forward task (or a vuln-migration-v1.1 patch milestone). The test test_vuln_detects_xss should remain in the failing set for M4 — counted as part of the 41 Cat-C entries that did NOT close in M4. Update the M4 stop-threshold to allow exactly 1 escalated regression (failure-count = 34, not 33) OR retire test_vuln_detects_xss to the Cat-B vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forward set (recommended: extend Cat-B to 34).
stop_threshold_impact: M4 final cargo test failure-count cannot reach the dispatch-contract target of 33 EXACTLY without either (a) silently fixing these regressions in active subcmd code (forbidden by no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_implementations), or (b) explicitly relabeling these 2 tests as Cat-B carry-forwards (recommended). This report documents the choice for operator review.
decision_pending_operator: Approve adding test_vuln_detects_xss + ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint to Cat-B carry-forward set, raising target to 35, OR fix the vuln-migration-v1 / field_access_info-v1 sink-bank / source-bank gaps in a separate atomic milestone before closing M4.
second_test_diagnosis: ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint expects AST-only mode to detect `cmd = gets` as a UserInput source in Ruby, then `IO.popen(cmd)` as a ShellExec sink. The test reports `[ast_only] expected at least one UserInput source; got sources=[]` — meaning Ruby's `gets` (a bareword call to a builtin) is not recognized by the AST-only source bank. The test comment notes 'Source side already AST-native; the failure point at HEAD is the sink' but the actual failure is on the SOURCE side, indicating the source bank for Ruby was further reduced or never wired for AST-only mode after vuln-migration-v1 / field_access_info-v1.
